mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals labor market cooling, prompting market participants to anticipate central bank policy pivots and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery regimes, an accelerating industrial production index reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC turns negative during identified recovery periods, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surges above 8%, markets price in aggressive monetary tightening and real yield spikes, triggering immediate negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 8.0, or signal loses predictive power after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months with payroll growth exceeding 150k, or rolling correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth outpaces retail sales growth indicates business investment leading consumer spending, signaling economic resilience and producing positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC increases), or 20-day returns during divergence periods are not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, simultaneous deceleration in industrial production and payroll growth signals demand exhaustion and rising recession probability, causing negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint deceleration signal shows no incremental predictive power over a single-series baseline.
